comprehension_gate.instruction: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION: Before any implementation work, read this entire prompt. Then present a structured summary to the Lead Architect: (a) Milestone objective in your own words (b) List of work items with 1-sentence descriptions (c) Files you will create or modify (d) Test strategy (e) Any risks or ambiguities you see Then STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval. Do NOT write code, create branches, or run commands until explicit approval is received.

milestone.title: Vikunja File-Based Bridge for Sandbox Agents
milestone.type: INFRASTRUCTURE — New tooling (no production service changes)
milestone.objective: Build a file-based bridge that enables sandboxed agents (Claude Cowork, Codex, or any agent without network access to localhost:3456) to read Vikunja task state and submit mutations through the workspace filesystem. The bridge consists of three components: (1) a state exporter that snapshots Vikunja into a JSON file, (2) an inbox processor that reads mutation requests from a JSON file and applies them to Vikunja via REST API, and (3) a daemon that runs both operations on a loop. The daemon runs on the host machine (which has network access to Vikunja) and watches workspace files that sandboxed agents can read/write.
milestone.background:
Claude Cowork runs agents inside a bwrap (bubblewrap) sandboxed Linux VM with hard network isolation. The sandbox cannot reach localhost:3456 (confirmed: curl returns HTTP 000). MCP tools from claude_desktop_config.json are NOT loaded in Cowork sessions. However, the workspace directory IS mounted into the sandbox as read/write.
This creates a clean transport opportunity: the host-side daemon writes state to a workspace file (sandbox reads it), and the sandbox writes mutation requests to a workspace file (host-side daemon reads and applies them).
The existing MCP server (tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py) and CLI (tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py) both hit Vikunja's REST API via httpx — the bridge daemon reuses the same HTTP helper pattern. No new dependencies are needed beyond what is already in the project (httpx).

item.title: Create bridge protocol specification
item.description:
Create `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/README.md` documenting the bridge protocol. This is the contract that sandboxed agents read to understand how to interact. Keep it lean — agents need to understand the protocol in under 30 seconds.
The README must define:
1. **State file** (`tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/state.json`): - Written by the host daemon, read-only for agents. - Contains: `exported_at` (ISO 8601 timestamp), `projects` (array of project summaries with task counts), `tasks` (array of task objects with id, title, done, priority, project_id, labels, description, comments). - Agents MUST treat this as a read-only snapshot. It may be up to N seconds stale (where N = poll interval, default 30s).
2. **Inbox file** (`tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/inbox.json`): - Written by agents, read and consumed by the host daemon. - JSON array of mutation request objects. Each object has: - `action` (string): one of `create_task`, `complete_task`, `add_comment`, `update_task`, `search_tasks`. - `params` (object): action-specific parameters (mirrors CLI/MCP tool params). - `request_id` (string): unique identifier (agent-generated UUID or timestamp). - After processing, the daemon moves processed requests to a `processed` array in `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/processed.json` with the result or error. - Agents append to the array. The daemon reads the full array, processes all entries, clears inbox.json back to `[]`, and writes results to processed.json.
3. **Processed file** (`tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/processed.json`): - Written by the host daemon after processing inbox entries. - JSON array of `{request_id, action, status: "ok"|"error", result|error}`. - Agents can check this to confirm their mutations were applied. - The daemon appends to this file (does not overwrite). Truncation is manual.
4. **Lock file** (`tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/.lock`): - NOT needed. The protocol is designed for single-writer-per-file: daemon writes state.json + processed.json, agent writes inbox.json. Race conditions are acceptable — the daemon polls, and if it reads a partial write it simply retries on the next cycle.

item.title: Create bridge daemon
item.description:
Create `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/daemon.py` — the host-side sync process.
Architecture: - Reuse the same auth/HTTP pattern from server.py (_get_token, _headers, _api_get, etc.) but DO NOT import from server.py (bridge must be standalone — no MCP dependency). Copy the HTTP helpers into a private module or inline them. - Single-threaded event loop: export state → check inbox → process inbox → sleep → repeat. - Default poll interval: 30 seconds (configurable via --interval CLI arg). - Auth: same env vars as server.py (VIKUNJA_URL, VIKUNJA_USER, VIKUNJA_PASS) with same defaults. Also loads .env from workspace root (same pattern as cli.py).
State export logic: - Fetch all projects via GET /api/v1/projects - For each project, fetch tasks via GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/tasks?per_page=200 - For each task, fetch comments via GET /api/v1/tasks/{id}/comments (only if task has comments — check `comment_count` field if available, else always fetch) - Build the state.json structure and write atomically (write to .tmp, rename). - Log: "Exported {N} projects, {M} tasks to state.json"
Inbox processing logic: - Read inbox.json. If empty array or file doesn't exist, skip. - For each request: - `create_task`: POST to /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/tasks (Vikunja uses PUT for create) - `complete_task`: POST to /api/v1/tasks/{task_id} with {"done": true} - `add_comment`: PUT to /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}/comments with {"comment": text} - `update_task`: POST to /api/v1/tasks/{task_id} with provided fields - `search_tasks`: GET /api/v1/tasks?s={query} — write results to processed.json - After processing all requests: - Write results to processed.json (append to existing array) - Clear inbox.json back to `[]` - Immediately re-export state.json so agents see the result on next read - Log each processed request: "Processed {action} request {request_id}: {status}"
Error handling: - If Vikunja is unreachable, log error and continue loop (do not crash). - If a single inbox request fails, record error in processed.json and continue processing remaining requests. - If inbox.json contains malformed JSON, log error, rename to inbox.json.bad, create fresh empty inbox.json.
CLI interface: - `python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.bridge.daemon` or `python tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/daemon.py` - Args: `--interval SECONDS` (default 30), `--once` (run one cycle and exit, for testing), `--verbose` (debug logging). - Startup banner: print bridge directory path, poll interval, Vikunja URL. - Graceful shutdown on Ctrl+C (KeyboardInterrupt).

item.title: Create bridge __init__.py and __main__.py
item.description: Create `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/__init__.py` (empty, makes it a package). Create `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/__main__.py` that imports and runs the daemon (enables `python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.bridge`).

item.title: Create startup script
item.description:
Create `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/start_bridge.bat` — one-click launcher for the Lead Architect (non-dev user). Pattern matches `tools/vikunja/start_vikunja.bat`.
Content: ```bat @echo off echo Starting Vikunja Bridge Daemon... echo Press Ctrl+C to stop. echo. cd /d "%~dp0..\..\..\" .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m tools.vikunja_mcp.bridge --verbose pause ```

item.title: Add VS Code task for bridge daemon
item.description:
Add a new task entry to `.vscode/tasks.json` (create the file if it doesn't exist, or add to the existing tasks array) so the bridge can be started from VS Code's Run Task menu:
```json { "label": "Run Vikunja Bridge Daemon", "type": "shell", "command": "${command:python.interpreterPath}", "args": ["-m", "tools.vikunja_mcp.bridge", "--verbose"], "isBackground": true, "options": { "cwd": "${workspaceFolder}" }, "problemMatcher": [] } ```
Check if `.vscode/tasks.json` already exists — if so, append to the `tasks` array. Do NOT overwrite existing tasks.

item.title: Initialize bridge data files
item.description:
Create the initial empty data files so the bridge directory is ready: - `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/state.json` — initial content: `{"exported_at": null, "projects": [], "tasks": []}` - `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/inbox.json` — initial content: `[]` - `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/processed.json` — initial content: `[]`
Add to `.gitignore`: ``` # Vikunja bridge runtime data (regenerated by daemon) tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/state.json tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/processed.json tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/inbox.json.bad ```
Do NOT gitignore inbox.json — agents write to it and it must be in the workspace for sandbox mount visibility. The empty `[]` initial state is safe to commit. state.json is gitignored because it contains task data that changes constantly. processed.json is gitignored for the same reason.
CORRECTION: On reflection, inbox.json should also be gitignored since it's transient runtime data. The README documents the protocol — agents don't need a committed inbox.json to know the format. The daemon creates the file if missing. Update: gitignore ALL three data files. The daemon must handle missing files gracefully (create them on first run).

item.title: Write tests for bridge daemon
item.description:
Create `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests/test_bridge.py` with unit tests.
Test strategy: mock httpx calls (no real Vikunja dependency). Use tmp_path fixture for bridge data files. Tests must be fast (no network, no sleep).
Required test cases:
1. `test_state_export_writes_valid_json` — mock API responses for 1 project with 2 tasks, verify state.json structure and field presence.
2. `test_state_export_atomic_write` — verify the daemon writes to .tmp then renames (check that a partial write doesn't corrupt state.json).
3. `test_inbox_create_task` — write a create_task request to inbox.json, run one daemon cycle, verify the correct API call was made and processed.json has the result.
4. `test_inbox_complete_task` — write a complete_task request, run one cycle, verify API call and processed.json.
5. `test_inbox_add_comment` — write an add_comment request, run one cycle, verify.
6. `test_inbox_malformed_json` — write garbage to inbox.json, run one cycle, verify inbox.json.bad is created and a fresh empty inbox.json replaces it.
7. `test_inbox_empty_is_noop` — empty inbox.json `[]`, run one cycle, verify no API calls made.
8. `test_vikunja_unreachable_continues` — mock httpx to raise ConnectError, verify daemon logs error and does not crash (returns gracefully from cycle).
9. `test_single_request_failure_continues` — inbox with 2 requests, first fails, verify second still processes and both appear in processed.json.
10. `test_processed_appends` — run two cycles with different requests, verify processed.json contains results from both cycles (append, not overwrite).
Test file location rationale: placing tests adjacent to the bridge code (tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests/) keeps the tooling self-contained. These are NOT production service tests — they don't belong in the root testpaths.
Add the test path to pyproject.toml testpaths so pytest discovers them: Add `"tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests"` to the testpaths array.

item.title: Update CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md bridge documentation
item.description:
Add a "Vikunja Bridge (Sandbox Agents)" section to both `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` explaining: - What the bridge is and when to use it (sandbox agents without MCP/network access) - Where to read task state: `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/state.json` - How to submit mutations: write to `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/inbox.json` - How to check results: read `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/processed.json` - Example inbox entry for common operations (create_task, complete_task, add_comment) - Note: bridge daemon must be running on host for mutations to apply
Keep it concise — 20-30 lines max per file. Agents need the protocol, not prose.

item.title: Update tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md
item.description:
Add a "File-Based Bridge" section to the existing README explaining the bridge architecture. Update the architecture diagram to show the bridge path:
``` Sandbox Agent ──(filesystem)──► bridge/inbox.json ──(daemon)──► Vikunja v2.3.0 ◄──(filesystem)── bridge/state.json ◄──(daemon)── localhost:3456 ```
Reference the bridge README for protocol details.

item.title: Record Entry 41 in Post-Operational Maturation Ledger
item.description:
Add Entry 41 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md documenting this milestone. Follow the existing entry format (see Entry 38-40 for reference).
Key fields: - Title: Infrastructure: Vikunja File-Based Bridge (COMPLETE) - Date: today's date - Branch: feature/vikunja-bridge - Predecessor: Entry 40 (Task 7/EA-1 Correction, commit on main) - Disposition: PASS - Type: INFRASTRUCTURE - Summary: Created file-based bridge for sandboxed agents to access Vikunja task management through workspace filesystem. Daemon exports state, processes inbox mutations, enables Claude Cowork and other sandboxed agents to participate in SDO/EA governance workflow without network access to Vikunja. - Files: list all created/modified files

context_notes.note: server.py and cli.py both implement the same auth/HTTP pattern: - _get_token() authenticates via POST /api/v1/login with username/password, caches JWT - _headers() returns Authorization: Bearer {token} - _api_get/put/post/delete wrap httpx calls with headers and timeout The bridge daemon should implement the same pattern standalone (no import from server.py).
cli.py has a _load_dotenv() function that reads .env from workspace root without requiring the python-dotenv package. The bridge daemon should reuse this exact pattern.
The bridge uses only httpx (already in project) and stdlib (json, pathlib, time, logging, argparse, shutil). No new pip installs needed.
On Windows, os.rename() fails if the destination exists. Use os.replace() instead for atomic write (write to .tmp, os.replace to final path). This is a known Windows-specific requirement.

The bridge daemon runs on the host and communicates with Vikunja at localhost:3456. No external network calls. No data leaves the machine. The bridge files are in the workspace which is mounted into the Cowork sandbox — this is local filesystem I/O only.

Adding tests to tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests/ and adding that path to pyproject.toml testpaths WILL change the test baseline. The EA must: 1. Run the REGRESSION gate BEFORE adding the new testpath to confirm current baseline. 2. Add the testpath and new tests. 3. Run REGRESSION gate again and record the new baseline. 4. Document the baseline change in the ledger entry.

Check if this file exists. The workspace has .vscode/settings.json and .vscode/mcp.json. tasks.json may or may not exist. If it does, preserve existing tasks and append. If not, create it with standard VS Code tasks.json structure.

gate.criteria: Module imports without error. All syntax valid.
All 10 bridge tests pass. Zero failures. Zero errors.

755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected (unchanged from pre-milestone baseline). Bridge tests are in a separate path and do NOT affect this gate unless the testpath was added to pyproject.toml — in that case, the new total should be 755 + {bridge_test_count} passed.

Daemon runs one cycle successfully: exports state.json with real Vikunja data (requires Vikunja running at localhost:3456), processes empty inbox, exits 0. If Vikunja is not running, this gate is SKIPPED (not FAILED) — the bridge tests (gate 2) cover the logic via mocks.

Only the following files should appear: - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/README.md (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/__init__.py (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/__main__.py (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/daemon.py (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/start_bridge.bat (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests/__init__.py (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests/test_bridge.py (new) - tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md (modified) - CLAUDE.md (modified) - AGENTS.md (modified) - .gitignore (modified) - .vscode/tasks.json (new or modified) - pyproject.toml (modified — testpaths addition) - docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (modified) No production service files (shared/, services/, launcher/) should appear.

verification.instruction: The Lead Architect will run these commands to verify milestone success. These are the ONLY confirmation mechanism — the Lead Architect cannot read code.

commands.expected: ~14 files as listed in FILES_CHECK gate above
10 passed (exact count may vary if EA adds edge-case tests)
commands.command: python -m pytest shared/ services/ tests/ --tb=short -q --no-header 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 3
commands.expected: Startup banner showing bridge directory and Vikunja URL, then export log line

commit_template.message:
infra: Vikunja file-based bridge for sandbox agents
- Created tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/ with daemon, protocol spec, and startup script - Bridge exports Vikunja state to state.json (host→sandbox, read-only) - Bridge processes inbox.json mutations (sandbox→host, append-only) - Results written to processed.json for agent confirmation - 10 unit tests (mocked HTTP, no Vikunja dependency) - Updated CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and vikunja_mcp README with bridge docs - Added VS Code task for one-click daemon launch - Added bridge test path to pyproject.toml testpaths
BRIDGE TESTS: {EA_FILLS_IN: e.g., 10 passed} REGRESSION gate: {EA_FILLS_IN: e.g., 755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected} Entry 41 recorded in Post-Operational Maturation Ledger.

attachments.instruction: Attach the following files when creating the EA session:

file.reason: Project governance, coding standards, gate-checking rules
Current pytest config — testpaths to update
Existing MCP server — HTTP helper pattern reference
Latest ledger entries for format reference
